bridge_lemma: Recast Σ_k 1/F_{n_k} as a controlled φ-base expansion via Binet: 1/F_n = √5 · φ^{-n} / (1 - (-1)^n φ^{-2n}), so the sum becomes a signed sparse series in φ^{-1}. Because φ is a Pisot number, Schmidt's 1980 theorem forces any element of Q(φ) to have an eventually periodic β-expansion (Parry 1960 / Frougny 1992 transducer normalization). Lacunarity n_{k+1}/n_k ≥ c > 1 forces unbounded gaps among the φ-atom supports, which cannot align with any finite-period block under φ-carry rules (φ^2 = φ + 1), contradicting eventual periodicity unless the tail is zero.
informal_proof_outline: Assume for contradiction that S := Σ_k 1/F_{n_k} ∈ Q for some lacunary index sequence (n_k) with n_{k+1}/n_k ≥ c > 1. Step 1 (Binet rewrite): Use the closed form 1/F_n = √5 · φ^{-n} / (1 - (-φ^{-2})^n) and expand the geometric factor as Σ_{j≥0} (-1)^{jn} φ^{-(2j+1)n}, so that √5 · S = Σ_k Σ_{j≥0} (-1)^{j n_k} φ^{-(2j+1) n_k}. This realizes √5 · S as a signed φ-power series whose support {(2j+1) n_k : k ∈ ℕ, j ∈ ℕ} is a structured but sparse subset of ℕ. Step 2 (Pisot algebraicity placement): Rationality of S forces √5 · S ∈ Q(√5) = Q(φ) as an algebraic integer over Q. Step 3 (Parry-Schmidt periodic β-expansion): Because φ is a Pisot number (algebraic integer > 1 with the unique Galois conjugate -1/φ of modulus < 1), Schmidt's theorem yields that every element of Q(φ) has an eventually periodic β-expansion in base φ. Equivalently, the φ-normalized digit stream of √5 · S in the Parry-admissible alphabet (no two consecutive 1's, golden-mean shift) must be eventually periodic. Step 4 (Carry-propagation lemma): Apply Frougny's finite transducer to normalize the raw signed digit stream from Step 1 into Parry-admissible form. The transducer is finite-state because φ is Pisot; its action on a single atom φ^{-m} sets up a carry chain of bounded depth using φ^2 = φ + 1. Across the lacunary support, however, the digit support gaps n_{k+1} - n_k grow without bound, and the transducer cannot maintain a periodic output block across an arbitrarily long stretch of zero input digits unless its state cycles trivially. Step 5 (Lacunarity ⟹ non-periodicity): Suppose the normalized expansion has eventual period P. The transducer's finite state pool means any cylinder of length > P of zeros in the input forces the output to enter a fixed periodic loop. The next non-zero atom (2j+1) n_{k+1} then injects a perturbation that the transducer must absorb in O(1) state changes, but the lacunary growth n_{k+1} ≥ c · n_k means this perturbation arrives at positions whose differences modulo P are not eventually periodic — a contradiction with eventual periodicity. Step 6 (Conclusion): Hence the tail must be eventually zero, but the support {n_k} is infinite, contradiction. Therefore S is irrational.
candidate_lemmas: binet_reciprocal_identity: For positive n, 1/F_n = √5 · φ^{-n} / (1 - (-φ^{-2})^n) holds in ℝ; equivalently 1/F_n = √5 · Σ_{j≥0} (-1)^{jn} φ^{-(2j+1)n}.
lacunary_phi_atom_support: For (n_k) with n_{k+1}/n_k ≥ c > 1, the support set {(2j+1) n_k : k, j ∈ ℕ} has gaps tending to infinity along the inner index k.
pisot_eventually_periodic_phi_expansion: Every element of Q(φ) has an eventually periodic φ-expansion in Parry-admissible (golden-mean shift) form (Schmidt 1980 / Parry 1960).
frougny_finite_transducer_phi: Normalization from any bounded signed alphabet to Parry-admissible φ-digits is realized by a finite-state transducer (Frougny 1992).
carry_propagation_bounded_depth: The φ-carry chain initiated by a single atom φ^{-m} has finite depth, using φ^2 = φ + 1, with depth bounded uniformly in m.

lacunary_periodicity_obstruction: A finite-state transducer cannot produce an eventually periodic output from an input whose nonzero-position differences are not eventually periodic modulo the output period.
honest_disclaimer: fit_score=0.5 reflects that the Pisot/β-expansion framing is the consensus top choice across the three-AI debate (Grok-4, Gemini, Codex/GPT-5.2 all converged on this lane in Round 2) and aligns with Codex F8's earlier independent identification of E267 as the #1 fusion-best-ROI target. Higher than E938's 0.3 because (i) the algebra is cleaner — a single number field Q(φ) replaces the moving-conductor Frey-Ribet conductor, (ii) the rationality⟺eventual-periodicity equivalence (Schmidt 1980) is a standing theorem rather than a multi-step machinery, and (iii) the carry-propagation lemma is the only genuinely open piece. The fusion lane CAN: hand Aristotle an explicit symbolic-dynamics reformulation that converts an Archimedean irrationality problem into a finite-state non-periodicity statement; the bridge_lemma is the heart and the candidate_lemmas are concrete. The fusion lane CANNOT: pre-validate that the lacunary-periodicity obstruction in Step 5 is provable for adversarial sequences with sub-quadratic c < 2 — this is precisely the open piece. Mathlib has Nat.fib and DFA infrastructure, but NO Pisot expansion API, NO Parry admissibility, NO finite-state transducers for non-integer bases. Aristotle would need to either synthesize these or find an alternative path. Codex/GPT-5.2's Round 2 self-revision noted the atoms expand to (2j+1)n exponents (not just n), so the symbolic object is richer than 'sparse digit stream'. The framing remains valid; the proof construction is non-trivial.
